game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Bring You Home:** Players do not control the protagonist Polo directly; instead, they manipulate the environment by dragging and dropping or tapping to rearrange pre-defined level segments (panels) on the screen. Polo automatically moves along the path created by these panels, and the objective is to arrange them to guide him safely past obstacles like spikes or enemies to the level's exit. Each level presents a unique set of panels containing various hazards and helpful elements, requiring players to strategically sequence them to form a continuous, safe route. Progression occurs by completing all levels within a "world" to unlock the next, with difficulty scaling through increased panel complexity and the introduction of new environmental mechanics. If Polo encounters a deadly obstacle, the player can tap a "rewind" button to undo his movement and attempt a different panel arrangement, with success defined by reaching the exit and failure by any fatal interaction.
game_info.description: Guide Polo safely home by rearranging the world around him! Each level consists of several panels containing paths, obstacles, and hazards. Your job is to drag and swap these panels to create a safe route from start to exit. Polo walks automatically along the path you create, so plan carefully! Avoid spikes, enemies, and gaps. If Polo meets danger, use the rewind button to try again. Complete all levels in each world to progress to harder challenges with more complex panel arrangements and new environmental mechanics.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←/→): Select different panels to swap
Space: Swap the two selected panels
Z: Start Polo walking on your arranged path
Shift: Rewind Polo to the start if he's in danger
Enter: Start the game from the title screen
Esc: Pause/Unpause the game
R: Restart and return to the title screen

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic panel manipulation mechanics. Validates that panels can be selected and swapped, and that Polo can navigate a simple path after arrangement.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Select panels systematically, swap them to create a basic path, then start Polo walking. Test that panel swapping works correctly and Polo follows the arranged path.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Polo should successfully navigate through rearranged panels without encountering obstacles. The test validates core mechanics of panel selection, swapping, and basic pathfinding.

TEST_1.expected: Polo should successfully navigate through rearranged panels without encountering obstacles. The test validates core mechanics of panel selection, swapping, and basic pathfinding.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the optimal strategy to complete all levels and win the game. Validates win condition achievement through strategic panel arrangement across increasing difficulty.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze each level's panel configuration, identify safe paths, systematically swap panels to create optimal routes, avoid all hazards, and guide Polo to exits efficiently. Progress through all worlds.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully complete all levels in all worlds, reaching GAME_OVER_WIN state. Validates that the game is winnable and all level progression mechanics work correctly.

TEST_2.expected: Successfully complete all levels in all worlds, reaching GAME_OVER_WIN state. Validates that the game is winnable and all level progression mechanics work correctly.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests the rewind mechanic and recovery from failure states. Validates that players can recover from mistakes and try different arrangements.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally create unsafe paths that lead Polo into hazards, then use rewind to reset his position. Test multiple rewind scenarios and verify state restoration.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Rewind successfully returns Polo to start position, allowing new panel arrangements. Game state properly resets without corruption, enabling continued gameplay.
